mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving equity valuations higher over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly expansion in real GDP and monthly housing starts signals robust aggregate demand and credit availability, which jointly lift corporate revenue expectations and drive positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of co-occurring expansion episodes, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a supply-constrained environment indicates successful capacity utilization and pricing power, which translates into higher profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO rises while input cost proxies remain elevated, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during recessionary contractions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading where year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5 percent triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations and anticipated central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth crosses above 3.5 percent
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 60% of instances following CPI prints above 3.5%, or forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant negative drift (p > 0.05) relative to control periods.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales reflects underlying consumer resilience, which sustains corporate cash flow projections and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 60-day forward return median remains below zero during consecutive months of RSAFS growth, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent nonfarm payroll growth above 150,000 and a stable unemployment rate below 5.5 percent indicate a healthy labor market without overheating, which supports earnings stability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of qualifying months, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes in a factor model.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production declines while real GDP remains positive signals a structural shift toward services and inventory drawdowns, which historically precedes equity corrections and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of divergence episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below chance levels (50%) over a 3-year walk-forward test.
